source: Vibe Gate 2.0 deterministic anti-patterns for Vibe UI execution checks

patterns.label: Default LLM indigo accent
patterns.bad: Uses #6366f1, #4f46e5, #818cf8, or #7c3aed as an unearned accent instead of the selected DESIGN.md palette.
patterns.fix: Replace the accent with a named color token from DESIGN.md, or document why the selected style explicitly supports it.
patterns.evidence: Matched a hardcoded default AI accent color.

patterns.label: Generic AI gradient
patterns.bad: Adds an arbitrary gradient, glow, neon, or glass effect that is not present in the selected design system.
patterns.fix: Use the selected DESIGN.md surface, border, and action color rules; keep gradients only when the source design documents them.
patterns.evidence: Matched gradient or effect utility classes.

patterns.label: Hardcoded color drift
patterns.bad: Introduces raw hex colors that do not appear in DESIGN.md.
patterns.fix: Replace raw values with DESIGN.md color tokens and reuse the same token for repeated roles.
patterns.evidence: Matched hex values outside the extracted DESIGN.md token set.

patterns.label: Too many accent colors
patterns.bad: Uses several unrelated accent colors, making the page feel like a palette collage.
patterns.fix: Keep one primary action/accent color and reserve secondary colors for semantic states or documented DESIGN.md roles.
patterns.evidence: Detected more raw colors than the active DESIGN.md appears to justify.

patterns.label: Low contrast text
patterns.bad: Uses very light text on light surfaces or muted text that likely fails readability.
patterns.fix: Increase contrast using documented foreground/body tokens and verify rendered contrast manually.
patterns.evidence: Matched low-contrast color combinations or opacity-heavy text styles.

patterns.label: Theme drift
patterns.bad: Mixes dark and light sections as a decorative theme flip without design-system reason.
patterns.fix: Use one dominant theme and reserve inversion for footer, code, product preview, or an explicit DESIGN.md pattern.
patterns.evidence: Matched repeated full-section dark/light inversions.

patterns.label: Emoji as UI icon
patterns.bad: Uses emoji as iconography, decoration, or product ornament without design-system permission.
patterns.fix: Use the project icon system, a real product asset, or plain text; keep emoji only if DESIGN.md explicitly allows it.
patterns.evidence: Matched Unicode emoji or symbol characters in UI code.

patterns.label: Fake proof or logo wall
patterns.bad: Uses customer/integration names as proof without evidence or ships neutral proof slots as if they were real logos.
patterns.fix: Use verified customer/integration evidence, neutral evidence categories, or an explicit placeholder note outside production UI.
patterns.evidence: Matched common brand/logo-wall names or proof-slot language.

patterns.label: Copied or official-sounding brand claim
patterns.bad: Implies official affiliation, endorsement, or brand-system ownership for an inspiration source.
patterns.fix: Use brand-safe inspiration language and avoid official claims, logos, trademarks, and copied screenshots.
patterns.evidence: Matched official/endorsed/partner language around inspiration references.

patterns.label: Visible design scaffold
patterns.bad: Renders internal design-read, dials, TODO, or implementation scaffold text in the production page.
patterns.fix: Keep design reads in .vibe-ui JSON, DESIGN.generated.md, comments, or reports rather than visible DOM.
patterns.evidence: Matched visible scaffold phrases such as Design read, dials, variance, or pre-flight.

patterns.label: Invented metric or claim
patterns.bad: Uses 10x, 99.9%, 100k+, millions, or similar proof claims without evidence.
patterns.fix: Cite a real source, use a verified metric, or rewrite as neutral product copy.
patterns.evidence: Matched unsupported numeric proof language.

patterns.label: Filler copy
patterns.bad: Ships placeholder text such as Lorem ipsum, your product, Acme, coming soon, or generic marketing filler.
patterns.fix: Write product-specific copy tied to the actual audience, workflow, and page goal.
patterns.evidence: Matched placeholder or generic copy markers.

patterns.label: Marketing buzzword stack
patterns.bad: Stacks generic words like seamless, unlock, transform, revolutionize, supercharge, beautiful, or next-generation without product evidence.
patterns.fix: Replace buzzwords with concrete product nouns, user actions, and evidence tied to the brief.
patterns.evidence: Matched repeated generic marketing terms.

patterns.label: Generic CTA
patterns.bad: Uses vague CTAs such as Get started, Learn more, Start free, or Book a demo without page-specific intent.
patterns.fix: Use a CTA that names the product action or evaluation moment, such as Review a release or Compare plans.
patterns.evidence: Matched generic CTA labels.

patterns.label: Weak proof strategy
patterns.bad: Relies on vague trust language without verified proof, evidence categories, screenshots, integrations, or user workflow proof.
patterns.fix: Choose a proof strategy before implementation: verified metrics, real integrations, customer logos, product evidence, or neutral evidence categories.
patterns.evidence: Matched trust/proof language without nearby evidence signals.

patterns.label: Generic role copy
patterns.bad: Names audiences without translating their real decision anxiety or workflow question.
patterns.fix: For each role, state the decision they need to make, the risk they fear, or the evidence they require.
patterns.evidence: Matched role names with generic can/track/manage/see copy.

patterns.label: Uppercase without tracking
patterns.bad: Uses uppercase text without explicit letter spacing or tracking.
patterns.fix: Add the selected style's tracking rule, or switch to sentence case.
patterns.evidence: Matched uppercase styling without letter-spacing or tracking.

patterns.label: Oversized H1
patterns.bad: Uses hero type so large that the first viewport becomes typographic spectacle instead of product communication.
patterns.fix: Match display type to the selected DESIGN.md scale and verify the hero fits mobile and desktop without crowding.
patterns.evidence: Matched very large H1 font sizes or viewport-scaling type.

patterns.label: Crushed tracking
patterns.bad: Applies extreme negative letter spacing that can make UI text brittle or illegible.
patterns.fix: Use the selected DESIGN.md tracking scale and reserve tight tracking for documented display styles.
patterns.evidence: Matched strongly negative letter-spacing.

patterns.label: Long reading line
patterns.bad: Allows paragraphs or article text to span too wide for comfortable reading.
patterns.fix: Constrain body copy with max-width and layout rhythm from DESIGN.md.
patterns.evidence: Matched wide text containers without max-width constraints.

patterns.label: Tiny text
patterns.bad: Uses text below 12px for meaningful labels, captions, or controls.
patterns.fix: Raise meaningful UI text to at least the selected caption/body-small token and verify mobile readability.
patterns.evidence: Matched font sizes below 12px.

patterns.label: Missing section identifiers
patterns.bad: Top-level sections are not labeled, making design review and iteration ambiguous.
patterns.fix: Add stable data-od-id values to top-level generated sections.
patterns.evidence: Matched section elements without data-od-id attributes.

patterns.label: Identical card grids
patterns.bad: Repeats equal card grids across multiple sections, creating a templated AI SaaS rhythm.
patterns.fix: Keep card grids where they aid comparison, but vary section jobs with walkthroughs, tables, product previews, role maps, or evidence strips.
patterns.evidence: Matched repeated repeat(N, 1fr) grid patterns or many same-shaped cards.

patterns.label: Nested cards
patterns.bad: Places cards inside decorative cards, reducing clarity and creating heavy UI nesting.
patterns.fix: Use a section layout plus individual cards, or a framed tool surface, but avoid card-inside-card decoration.
patterns.evidence: Matched nested card class/selector patterns.

patterns.label: Repeated section kicker
patterns.bad: Every section starts with the same eyebrow/kicker pattern, creating mechanical AI rhythm.
patterns.fix: Use kickers only where they clarify hierarchy; vary with direct headings, tabs, filters, or product state labels.
patterns.evidence: Matched repeated eyebrow/kicker classes or uppercase section labels.

patterns.label: Hero eyebrow chip
patterns.bad: Adds a rounded hero chip as decorative novelty without product information.
patterns.fix: Use a plain kicker, status label, product proof, or remove the chip unless DESIGN.md explicitly uses it.
patterns.evidence: Matched hero pill/chip/badge patterns.

patterns.label: Numbered section markers
patterns.bad: Uses 01/02/03 markers as decoration instead of meaningful sequence or navigation.
patterns.fix: Use numbers only for actual ordered workflows, steps, or indexed content.
patterns.evidence: Matched decorative numeric section labels.

patterns.label: Icon tile above heading
patterns.bad: Repeats an icon tile above every heading/card, a common AI-generated feature-card tell.
patterns.fix: Use icons only when they encode a real product concept, or replace with product states, diagrams, or text hierarchy.
patterns.evidence: Matched repeated icon/tile/card heading stacks.

patterns.label: Unsupported heavy shadow
patterns.bad: Adds heavy elevation that most selected DESIGN.md systems do not call for.
patterns.fix: Use the documented shadow token or reduce to a subtle border/shadow treatment.
patterns.evidence: Matched heavy shadow utility classes or large box-shadow values.

patterns.label: Radius drift
patterns.bad: Uses arbitrary large radii that conflict with the selected component system.
patterns.fix: Use the DESIGN.md radius scale consistently across cards, buttons, panels, and inputs.
patterns.evidence: Matched arbitrary rounded utilities or large border-radius values.

patterns.label: Fake screenshot divs
patterns.bad: Builds product proof from generic skeleton bars and fake dashboard divs instead of meaningful product state.
patterns.fix: Use a real screenshot, product-owned mock, or concrete UI state with readable labels and domain data.
patterns.evidence: Matched repeated skeleton bars or fake-app/dashboard placeholder structures.

patterns.label: Unlabeled button
patterns.bad: Renders a button without accessible text or an aria-label.
patterns.fix: Add visible text or a precise aria-label that names the action.
patterns.evidence: Matched button elements without a detectable text label or aria-label.

patterns.label: Small touch target
patterns.bad: Uses interactive controls that may be too small for touch input.
patterns.fix: Give buttons, tabs, and inputs stable dimensions with at least the selected design's practical touch target.
patterns.evidence: Matched very small button/input heights or tight padding.

patterns.label: Clipped overflow
patterns.bad: Uses hidden overflow on content containers where text or controls may be clipped.
patterns.fix: Reserve overflow hidden for media/framed surfaces and verify text wrapping at mobile and desktop widths.
patterns.evidence: Matched overflow-hidden or overflow: hidden on likely content containers.

patterns.label: Weak component structure
patterns.bad: The target does not look like reusable page or component code.
patterns.fix: Use semantic sections, repeated components, and explicit hierarchy before styling details.
patterns.evidence: Static scan found too few component/page structure signals.
